reasoning: The reference class is short-to-medium-term fluctuations in the 10-year vs 2-year Treasury yield spread. The most recent data point (2025-08-15) was 0.58, which is uniquely high relative to the short-term mean over the past month (roughly 0.50-0.54). Because 0.58 is a local spike, short-term mean reversion suggests the probability of exceeding this value by August 24 is low (around 33%), consistent with the FRED statistical model's indication of ~30%. As the forecast horizon increases, the random walk variance increases, diluting the effect of the local extreme starting value and pushing the probability closer to 0.5. Moreover, the general multi-month macroeconomic context of yield curve steepening during and after Fed rate cuts introduces a positive drift, which slightly biases the longer-term probabilities (6 months relative to 1 month) upwards, landing near 52% for February 2026.
